title: Dactylorchis occidentalis
intro: Dactylorchis occidentalis, also known as Western Autumn Lady's Tresses, is a rare orchid species found in North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorchis occidentalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is also used in dried form to make capsules or teas, although its use is not well-documented and more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
